title: Marcelo Ortiz M.
text:
I’m an Assistant Professor at Universitat Pompeu Fabra, and am affiliated with the Barcelona School of Economics and the Barcelona School of Management.
My research in corporate governance and accounting examines how regulatory interventions, such as disclosure regulation and taxation, affect corporate strategy and market outcomes.
My work has been published in leading academic journals in accounting, finance, and management, and has been referenced in policy discussions by the IASB and the UK Endorsement Board (UKEB), the UK body responsible for endorsing IFRS.
I also hold a Ramón y Cajal Fellowship from the Spanish Ministry for Science and Innovation, and co-organize the Barcelona Accounting Summer Workshops.
Keywords: Corporate Governance, Disclosure Regulation, Corporate and Inheritance Taxation, Private firms.
CV link.
Spotlight:

I will be serving as an Academic Visitor at the Saïd Business School, University of Oxford, this spring.
New Working Paper: Board Gender Quotas and Female CEOs in Private Firms.
Updated Working Paper: The Effect of Mandatory Disclosure on the CEO Labor Market.
Policy Impact: Our findings on the role of transparency thresholds for M&As (Review Financial Studies, 2023) were incorporated into the discussion of IFRS 3 Business Combinations by the UK Endorsement Board and analyzed in an IASB Meeting.
Upcoming presentations: Warwick Business School, Lancaster University, EAA Annual Congress 2026, SIOE 2026, FMARC 2026, and “Ownership, Control and Performance” Conference (PSL Dauphine, 2026), EFA Annual Meeting 2026.
Recent presentations: SKEMA Business School, CUNEF University, St. Gallen Financial Economics Workshop, PUC-Chile, Paris-Dauphine Family Firm Seminars.

title: About
text:
I grew up in Chile, moving around Viña del Mar and Santiago, an area renowned for its beautiful valleys, snowy mountains, and stunning coastline.
I completed all my degrees at Universidad Adolfo Ibañez, starting with an undergraduate degree in industrial engineering, followed by a Master of Science in Financial Engineering, and then a PhD in Finance. When I wasn’t studying, I spent most of my weekends engaged in water sports, hiking, and photography.
I moved to the Netherlands in 2018 as a visiting PhD student at Erasmus University. In 2019, I started working at Universitat Pompeu Fabra.
Before starting my PhD, I worked as a financial trader for five years, handling interest and liquidity risk exposure in Itau Bank (formerly “Corpbanca”).
